zh.lifeSummary: 當John de Clinton, 2nd Lord Clinton於大約1300年出生在英國英格蘭瓦維其可爾可爾希爾時，他的父親John de Clinton44歲，母親Ida Odingsells37歲。他於1316年2月28日與Margery Corbet在英國英格蘭武斯特斯爾結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和3個女兒的父母。他去世於1335年4月1日之前在英國英格蘭瓦維其可爾馬可斯托可，埋葬在英國英格蘭瓦維其可爾可爾希爾。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Clinton,
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Irish (Louth, Westmeath) and Scottish: shortened form of   McClinton  .

English: habitational name, either from Glympton in Oxfordshire, named as ‘settlement (Old English tūn) on the Glym river’, a Celtic river name meaning ‘bright stream’, or from Glinton in Northamptonshire, early recorded as Clinton (named with an unrecorded Old English element akin to Middle Low German glinde ‘enclosure, fence’ + Old English tūn). The surname was taken to Ireland in or before the 16th century.

History: Charles Clinton (born 1690 in Longford, Ireland) organized a group of colonists and founded the settlement of Little Britain, Ulster County, NY, in 1731. His son George Clinton (1739–1812) was governor of NY (1777–95), and they had many prominent descendants. — An English aristocratic family called Clinton was founded by Geoffrey de Clinton (flourished 1130), who held lands at Glympton, Oxfordshire. He was chamberlain and treasurer to Henry I (1100–35).

variants.name: McClinton
meanings: Irish (northern) and Scottish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Gille Fhionntain (Scottish), Mac Giolla Fhionntáin (Irish) ‘son of the servant (i.e. devotee) of (Saint) Fionntan’, whose name has been explained as ‘the white ancient’.

nameMeanings.name: John
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: As a boy's name this represents a revival of Middle English Jan, a by‐form of John , or an adoption of the common European form with this spelling. As a girl's name it is a short form of names such as Janet and Janice .

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

en.lifeSummary: When John de Clinton, 2nd Lord Clinton was born about 1300, in Coleshill, Warwickshire, England, United Kingdom, his father, John de Clinton, was 44 and his mother, Ida Odingsells, was 37. He married Margery Corbet on 28 February 1316, in Worcestershire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 3 daughters. He died before 1 April 1335, in Maxstoke, Warwickshire, England, United Kingdom, and was buried in Coleshill, Warwickshire, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque John de Clinton, 2nd Lord Clinton est né environ 1300, à Coleshill, Warwickshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, John de Clinton, avait 44 ans et sa mère, Ida Odingsells, avait 37 ans. Il a épousé Margery Corbet le 28 février 1316, à Worcestershire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils et 3 filles. Il est décédé avant 1 avril 1335, à Maxstoke, Warwickshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, et a été enterré à Coleshill, Warwickshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

Er hat am 28. Februar 1316 in Worcestershire, England, United Kingdom Margery Corbet geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Söhne und 3 Töchter. Er starb vor 1. ja.lifeSummary: John de Clinton, 2nd Lord Clintonが約1300年にイギリスイギリスワーウィクシュアイアコーレシュイルで生まれた時，父John de Clintonは44歳，母Ida Odingsellsは37歳であった。1316年2月28日にイギリスイギリスウオルセステルシュアイアでMargery Corbetと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子と3人の娘の親であった。彼は1335年4月1日以前にイギリスイギリスワーウィクシュアイアマクスストクで死去し，イギリスイギリスワーウィクシュアイアコーレシュイルにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: John de Clinton, 2nd Lord Clinton(이)가 대략 1300년 영국 잉글랜드 워윅실르 콜레시에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 John de Clinton(은)는 44세였고, 그의 어머니 Ida Odingsells(은)는 37세였다. 그는 1316년 2월 28일 영국 잉글랜드 워스스터실르에서 Margery Corbet(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1335년 4월 1일 이전 영국 잉글랜드 워윅실르 맥스스토크에서 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 워윅실르 콜레시에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando John de Clinton, 2nd Lord Clinton nasceu aproximadamente 1300, em Coleshill, Warwickshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, seu pai, John de Clinton, tinha 44 anos e sua mãe, Ida Odingsells, tinha 37 anos. Ele casou-se com Margery Corbet em 28 de fevereiro de 1316, em Worcestershire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos e 3 filhas. Ele faleceu antes 1 de abril de 1335, em Maxstoke, Warwickshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, e foi sepultado em Coleshill, Warwickshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда John de Clinton, 2nd Lord Clinton родился около 1300 г. в местности колешилл, варвиккшир, Англия, Великобритания, его отцу по имени John de Clinton было 44 года, а матери, которую звали Ida Odingsells, 37. Он женился на женщине по имени Margery Corbet 28 февраля 1316 г. в местности ворсестиршир, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 3 дочери. Он умер перед 1 апрель 1335 г. в местности макссток, варвиккшир, Англия, Великобритания и был похоронен в местности колешилл, варвиккшир, Англия, Великобритания.
